trial_name: A Multicenter, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase 2 Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Investigational Agent NVB-7734 in Combination with Platinum-Based Doublet Chemotherapy in Subjects with Previously Untreated Stage III Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (NSCLC) Who Are Not Candidates for Surgical Resection

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Patient must be aged 18 years or older.
Histologically or cytologically confirmed diagnosis of NSCLC, Stage IIIA (T4N0-1, T3N2, or T4N2 per AJCC 8th edition), Stage IIIB, or Stage IIIC.

Stage IIIA patients deemed resectable by the treating thoracic surgeon are not eligible; patients initially evaluated for surgery but determined unresectable by a multidisciplinary tumor board are eligible with documentation.
Subject must have at least one measurable lesion per RECIST v1.1; previously irradiated lesions are not considered measurable unless demonstrated progression since radiation (and not in brain).
ECOG performance status must be 0 or 1.

Patients with ECOG PS 2 may be considered case-by-case with medical monitor discussion and approval if PS2 is judged attributable to disease burden rather than comorbidities, capped at 10% of enrollment.
No prior systemic therapy for NSCLC allowed, except prior adjuvant/neoadjuvant chemotherapy completed more than 12 months prior to Stage III diagnosis with documented interval.

Prior radiation therapy to the chest is permitted if at least 4 weeks have elapsed since last fraction, toxicities resolved to Grade 1 or less (except alopecia), and there is a measurable lesion outside the radiation field or a lesion within the field that has unequivocally progressed.
Absolute neutrophil count must be >= 1,500/uL.
Platelet count must be >= 100,000/uL.
Hemoglobin must be >= 9.0 g/dL; transfusions permitted if administered more than 14 days prior to first dose.
Serum creatinine <= 1.5x ULN or creatinine clearance >= 50 mL/min (Cockcroft-Gault); patients receiving cisplatin must have CrCl >= 60 mL/min.
AST and ALT <= 2.5x ULN, or <= 5x ULN if suspicious but unconfirmed hepatic lesions are being followed.
Total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN; patients with known Gilbert's syndrome may have bilirubin up to 3.0 mg/dL.
INR or PT and aPTT <= 1.5x ULN, unless subject is on anticoagulant therapy with coagulation parameters within therapeutic range.
Life expectancy of greater than 3 months in the opinion of the investigator.
Female subjects of childbearing potential must have a negative serum or urine pregnancy test within 72 hours prior to first dose.

Women of childbearing potential and male subjects with partners of childbearing potential must agree to use at least one highly effective method of contraception from screening through 6 months after last dose.

Subject must provide a fresh tumor biopsy obtained within 60 days of randomization (preferred) or an archival tumor specimen (FFPE block or at least 15 unstained slides) obtained within 3 years with no intervening systemic therapy; if neither available, enrollment may proceed with sponsor medical monitor approval and PD-L1 stratification defaults to unknown.

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Mixed small-cell and non-small-cell histology, or any component of small-cell lung cancer.
Known EGFR sensitizing mutation (exon 19 deletion, L858R, or other known sensitizing mutations) or ALK rearrangement.
Known ROS1 rearrangement.

Active or prior autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment within the past 2 years, excluding replacement therapy (thyroxine, insulin, physiologic corticosteroids) and excluding vitiligo, resolved childhood asthma/atopy, or well-controlled type 1 diabetes.

Known active CNS metastases; previously treated brain metastases are permitted if clinically stable for >=4 weeks, no new/enlarging lesions on MRI within 28 days of first dose, and off CNS-indicated corticosteroids (>10mg/day prednisone equivalent) for >=14 days.
Leptomeningeal disease, regardless of other CNS metastasis status.

History of non-infectious pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease requiring steroids, or current pneumonitis/ILD; history of fully resolved radiation pneumonitis without ongoing corticosteroid requirement is not exclusionary.
Receipt of a live or live-attenuated vaccine within 30 days of first dose (inactivated vaccines permitted).

Known HIV infection with CD4 count <350 cells/uL or detectable viral load within past 6 months; HIV-positive subjects with adequate immune function on stable ART >4 weeks with undetectable viral load are permitted.

Known active hepatitis B (HBsAg positive and/or detectable HBV DNA) or hepatitis C (detectable HCV RNA); prior HBV infection with undetectable HBV DNA or cured HCV with undetectable RNA are permitted.
Active infection requiring systemic therapy; subjects must have completed IV antibiotics >=7 days prior to first dose (oral antibiotics for non-serious infections permitted).

Significant cardiovascular disease within 6 months of screening, including myocardial infarction, unstable angina, CVA/TIA, NYHA Class III or IV heart failure, or uncontrolled hypertension (systolic >160 mmHg or diastolic >100 mmHg despite optimal management).
QTcF interval >470 ms (females) or >450 ms (males) at screening.
Second- or third-degree heart block unless a pacemaker is in place.

Known additional malignancy that is active and/or progressive requiring treatment, excluding basal cell carcinoma, curatively treated cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma, carcinoma in situ, or malignancies considered cured with minimal recurrence risk (e.g., low-grade prostate cancer on active surveillance); borderline cases require sponsor medical monitor consultation.
Prior organ or allogeneic stem cell transplant; prior autologous stem cell transplant is permitted if >6 months have elapsed with no active graft-related toxicity.
Known hypersensitivity to any component of NVB-7734 formulation (histidine, sucrose, polysorbate 80) or severe (Grade >=3) hypersensitivity to any monoclonal antibody.

Concurrent enrollment in another interventional clinical trial; subjects who received an investigational agent must complete a washout of at least 28 days or 5 half-lives (whichever is shorter) prior to first dose. Observational/registry study participation is permitted.

trial_name: VIVANTRA-HER2 STUDY: A Phase 2, Open-Label, Single-Arm Study Evaluating VT-4019 (Vivantra) in Combination with Docetaxel in HER2-Positive Locally Advanced or Metastatic Breast Cancer Patients Naive to Anti-HER2 Therapy

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Age >= 18 years (or age of majority in country, whichever is higher)
Confirmed HER2-positive disease by local laboratory testing: IHC 3+, or IHC 2+ with confirmatory ISH showing HER2/CEP17 ratio >= 2.0 or average HER2 gene copy number >= 6.0 signals/cell
If local HER2 testing is IHC 2+/ISH equivocal (ratio 1.8-2.2 or copy number 4.0-6.0), central laboratory confirmation of HER2 status is required before study treatment can begin
Disease must be locally advanced (not amenable to curative surgery or radiation) or metastatic (Stage IV); de novo metastatic presentation is acceptable

For locally advanced disease, patient must have been evaluated by a surgical oncologist and/or radiation oncologist and determined not to be a candidate for curative-intent local therapy, with documentation in the chart

Patient must not have received any prior anti-HER2 directed therapy (trastuzumab, pertuzumab, T-DM1, trastuzumab deruxtecan, lapatinib, neratinib, tucatinib, margetuximab, or other HER2-targeted agent) in any setting (adjuvant, neoadjuvant, or advanced), EXCEPT that prior adjuvant/neoadjuvant trastuzumab and/or pertuzumab for early-stage disease is allowed if the last dose was administered more than 12 months before documentation of advanced/metastatic disease AND recurrence is not considered refractory (did not occur during or within 6 months of completing adjuvant/neoadjuvant therapy); any prior T-DM1, T-DXd, or HER2 TKI in any setting remains exclusionary with no washout provision; ambiguous cases require medical monitor contact
No prior systemic chemotherapy for advanced/metastatic breast cancer allowed
Prior hormonal therapy for HR+/HER2+ advanced disease is allowed only if given as single agent without concurrent chemotherapy or anti-HER2 therapy, discontinued at least 7 days before first dose

Prior chemotherapy in the adjuvant/neoadjuvant setting is permitted if completed >= 6 months before enrollment, with toxicities resolved to Grade 1 or baseline (except alopecia and peripheral neuropathy which must be <= Grade 2)

At least one measurable lesion per RECIST 1.1; bone-only disease is acceptable only with at least one lytic or mixed lytic-blastic lesion with measurable soft-tissue component >= 10mm; exclusively blastic bone disease is not eligible
ECOG Performance Status 0-1 (PS 2 patients are not eligible under any circumstance)
Absolute neutrophil count >= 1500/mm3, assessed within 14 days of first dose
Platelets >= 100,000/mm3, assessed within 14 days of first dose
Hemoglobin >= 9 g/dL with no transfusion within 7 days prior to assessment
Serum creatinine <= 1.5 x ULN OR calculated creatinine clearance >= 50 mL/min (Cockcroft-Gault)
Total bilirubin <= ULN (or <= 2.0 x ULN for patients with documented Gilbert syndrome)
AST/ALT <= 2.5 x ULN (or <= 5 x ULN if hepatic metastases present)
LVEF >= 50% by echocardiogram or MUGA scan, performed within 28 days of first dose; if echo and MUGA results are discrepant, use the echo value
Patients on anticoagulation are eligible if on a stable dose for >= 2 weeks with INR/aPTT within therapeutic range

Women of reproductive potential must have a negative pregnancy test within 72 hours prior to first dose and agree to use effective contraception during treatment and for 7 months after last VT-4019 dose or 6 months after last docetaxel dose, whichever is later
Men with female partners of reproductive potential must use barrier contraception during treatment and for 4 months after last dose

Availability of tumor tissue: willing to undergo fresh biopsy if archival tissue unavailable; archival tissue acceptable if obtained within 6 months of enrollment with no intervening systemic anticancer therapy; minimum 10 unstained FFPE slides or a tissue block required (sponsor approval allows enrollment without tissue if inaccessible, though not evaluable for biomarker endpoints)

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: HER2-low disease (IHC 1+, IHC 2+/ISH negative, or IHC 0) is excluded

Known ER+ and/or PR+ disease that has not been treated with at least one line of endocrine therapy is excluded unless there is a documented contraindication to endocrine therapy or rapidly progressive visceral crisis requiring immediate chemotherapy

Active CNS metastases are excluded; leptomeningeal disease is excluded; treated and stable brain metastases are eligible only if definitive treatment completed >= 4 weeks prior to first dose, no progression on post-treatment brain MRI, and off dexamethasone >= 2 weeks or on stable dose <= 2mg/day dexamethasone equivalent

Inflammatory breast cancer (T4d) with purely inflammatory presentation and no measurable disease beyond skin involvement is excluded; patients with measurable disease beyond skin involvement may be enrolled

Significant cardiac history or risk excluded: CHF NYHA Class >= II, myocardial infarction within 6 months, unstable angina, clinically significant uncontrolled arrhythmia, congenital long QT syndrome, history of LVEF decline to <40% on any prior therapy, or QTcF > 480ms at screening
Prior cumulative anthracycline exposure exceeding 300 mg/m2 doxorubicin-equivalent dose (using specified conversion factors) is excluded

Known additional active malignancy is excluded, except adequately treated non-melanoma skin cancer, cervical CIS, DCIS treated with curative intent, or malignancy diagnosed > 3 years ago without recurrence
Concurrent low-grade malignancies (e.g., indolent lymphoma on watch-and-wait) should be discussed with the medical monitor for eligibility determination
Patients requiring systemic immunosuppression (>10mg/day prednisone or equivalent) are excluded; inhaled/topical steroids and adrenal replacement doses are permitted
Active serious infection requiring parenteral antibiotics or antifungals within 14 days of first dose is excluded

Known HIV, HBV, or HCV infection is not exclusionary if adequately controlled (HIV: undetectable viral load on ART with CD4 >200; HBV: on suppressive therapy with undetectable DNA; HCV: completed treatment with SVR or undetectable RNA)

Hypersensitivity to any component of VT-4019 or docetaxel, or to drugs formulated with polysorbate 80, or history of severe (Grade >=3) infusion-related reaction to any monoclonal antibody therapy is excluded
Pregnant or breastfeeding women are excluded
Major surgery within 4 weeks of first dose or planned major surgery during the study period is excluded; minor procedures performed >= 7 days prior are not exclusionary

Prior radiation therapy is excluded unless completed >= 2 weeks prior to first dose with all radiation-related toxicities resolved to <= Grade 1 (except alopecia), and radiation was not administered to the only target lesion unless that lesion has subsequently progressed

Concurrent participation in another interventional clinical trial is excluded; prior investigational agent must be discontinued >= 28 days or 5 half-lives (whichever is shorter, minimum 14 days) before first dose

Known allergy to any excipient of VT-4019 formulation (L-histidine, L-histidine HCl monohydrate, alpha-alpha-trehalose dihydrate, polysorbate 20, water for injection) is excluded from receiving VT-4019

trial_name: A Randomized, Open-Label, Phase 3 Study Comparing Anti-EGFR Monoclonal Antibody CET-880 in Combination with FOLFOX Versus FOLFOX Alone as First-Line Treatment in Subjects with KRAS Wild-Type, RAS Wild-Type Metastatic Colorectal Cancer

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Patient must be aged 18 years or older at the time of informed consent.
Histologically or cytologically confirmed adenocarcinoma of the colon or rectum with documented metastatic disease (Stage IV per AJCC 8th edition).

Confirmed KRAS wild-type (codons 12, 13, 59, 61, 117, 146) and NRAS wild-type status by validated molecular assay performed on tumor tissue from the primary or metastatic site; results must be available prior to randomization.

No prior systemic chemotherapy for metastatic colorectal cancer; prior adjuvant chemotherapy (fluoropyrimidine-based with or without oxaliplatin) is permitted if completed >= 6 months prior to documentation of metastatic disease.
At least one measurable lesion per RECIST v1.1; previously irradiated lesions are not considered measurable unless documented progression after radiation.
ECOG performance status 0 or 1.
Absolute neutrophil count >= 1,500/uL, platelets >= 100,000/uL, hemoglobin >= 9.0 g/dL.
Serum creatinine <= 1.5x ULN or creatinine clearance >= 50 mL/min (Cockcroft-Gault).
AST and ALT <= 3x ULN (or <= 5x ULN if liver metastases are present); total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN.
Adequate coagulation function: INR <= 1.5x ULN and aPTT <= 1.5x ULN unless on therapeutic anticoagulation.
Serum magnesium >= lower limit of normal; supplementation permitted prior to enrollment.

Left-sided primary tumor (splenic flexure, descending colon, sigmoid colon, or rectum) preferred; right-sided primary tumors (cecum, ascending colon, hepatic flexure, transverse colon) are eligible but stratified separately and the investigator must confirm clinical suitability for anti-EGFR therapy in the context of known inferior outcomes.

Female patients of childbearing potential must have a negative serum pregnancy test within 7 days prior to first dose and agree to effective contraception throughout treatment and for 6 months after last dose.
Life expectancy > 12 weeks in the investigator's clinical judgment.

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Known BRAF V600E mutation; BRAF-mutant patients are excluded due to expected poor response to anti-EGFR monotherapy backbone.
Microsatellite instability-high (MSI-H) or mismatch repair deficient (dMMR) tumors, unless the patient has already received and progressed on prior checkpoint inhibitor therapy.
Prior anti-EGFR therapy (cetuximab, panitumumab, or any investigational EGFR-targeting agent) in any setting.
Known grade 3 or higher hypersensitivity to cetuximab, murine proteins, or any component of the CET-880 formulation.
Known peripheral neuropathy >= Grade 2 (CTCAE v5.0); prior oxaliplatin-related neuropathy must have resolved to Grade 1 or lower.
Known dihydropyrimidine dehydrogenase (DPD) deficiency; DPD testing is recommended but not mandated prior to enrollment.
Active or untreated brain metastases; treated and stable CNS lesions are permitted if asymptomatic and off steroids >= 2 weeks.
Significant cardiovascular disease within 6 months: myocardial infarction, unstable angina, NYHA Class III-IV heart failure, or cerebrovascular accident.
Active inflammatory bowel disease or chronic diarrhea (>= 4 loose stools/day at baseline) that, in the investigator's judgment, would impair tolerance of oxaliplatin-based chemotherapy.
Concurrent enrollment in another interventional trial or receipt of investigational agent within 28 days or 5 half-lives prior to first dose.

trial_name: MERIDIAN-IO: A Phase 2, Randomized, Double-Blind Study of Anti-PD-1 Antibody Pembrova Combined with Anti-LAG-3 Antibody LG-2250 Versus Pembrova Plus Placebo in Patients with Unresectable or Metastatic Melanoma Not Previously Treated with Anti-PD-1/PD-L1 Therapy

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Age >= 18 years at the time of signing informed consent.
Histologically confirmed unresectable Stage III or Stage IV cutaneous, acral, or mucosal melanoma per AJCC 8th edition; uveal melanoma is excluded.

No prior systemic therapy for unresectable or metastatic melanoma; prior adjuvant or neoadjuvant immunotherapy (anti-PD-1, anti-CTLA-4, or combination) is permitted if completed >= 6 months prior to documentation of unresectable/metastatic disease and no immune-related adverse event requiring systemic immunosuppression persists.
Known BRAF V600 mutation status by validated assay; BRAF-mutant and BRAF wild-type patients are both eligible but stratified at randomization.
At least one measurable lesion per RECIST v1.1.
ECOG performance status 0 or 1.
Absolute neutrophil count >= 1,500/uL, platelets >= 100,000/uL, hemoglobin >= 9.0 g/dL.
AST/ALT <= 2.5x ULN (or <= 5x ULN with hepatic metastases); total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN (or <= 3x ULN with Gilbert syndrome).
Serum creatinine <= 1.5x ULN or eGFR >= 40 mL/min/1.73m2 (CKD-EPI).
Serum LDH must be documented at baseline for stratification; elevated LDH does not preclude enrollment.
Willing to provide a pre-treatment tumor biopsy (fresh preferred, archival acceptable if obtained within 12 months with no intervening systemic therapy) for PD-L1 and LAG-3 expression analysis.
Female patients of childbearing potential must have a negative pregnancy test within 72 hours prior to first dose and agree to effective contraception through 120 days after last dose.

Patients with asymptomatic brain metastases may be enrolled if lesions are <= 2 cm, no perilesional edema on MRI, and not requiring corticosteroids; the investigator must confirm neurological stability with documentation.

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Uveal (ocular) melanoma.
Prior therapy with any anti-PD-1, anti-PD-L1, anti-LAG-3, or anti-CTLA-4 antibody for unresectable or metastatic disease.

Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive treatment within 2 years; replacement therapy (thyroxine, insulin, physiologic corticosteroids <= 10 mg/day prednisone equivalent) is permitted.
History of immune-related adverse event >= Grade 3 from prior checkpoint inhibitor therapy that has not resolved to Grade 1 or better, except endocrinopathies managed with stable replacement.
Symptomatic brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease; requirement for corticosteroids > 10 mg/day prednisone equivalent for CNS indications.
Active infection requiring systemic antibiotics, antifungals, or antivirals within 7 days of first dose.
Receipt of live or live-attenuated vaccine within 30 days of first dose.
Known HIV with detectable viral load or CD4 < 350 cells/uL; known active HBV or HCV infection.
History of organ transplantation (solid organ or allogeneic stem cell) requiring ongoing immunosuppression.
Concurrent enrollment in another interventional clinical trial; prior investigational agent must have been discontinued >= 28 days before first dose.

trial_name: PANTERA-1: A Phase 2, Open-Label, Single-Arm Study of Gemcitabine Plus Nab-Paclitaxel in Combination with Novel Stromal Disrupting Agent SDA-4412 in Patients with Locally Advanced, Unresectable Pancreatic Ductal Adenocarcinoma

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Age >= 18 years at enrollment.
Histologically or cytologically confirmed pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC); neuroendocrine tumors, acinar cell carcinoma, and other non-ductal histologies are excluded.

Locally advanced, unresectable disease as determined by a multidisciplinary panel including a hepatobiliary/pancreatic surgeon, using NCCN criteria for unresectability (involvement of >180 degrees of the SMA or celiac axis, unreconstructible SMV/portal vein involvement, or aortic involvement).

Borderline resectable cases may be considered if the patient has been evaluated by a surgical oncologist and deemed not a candidate for upfront resection; these cases require sponsor medical monitor approval prior to enrollment.
No prior systemic therapy for pancreatic cancer; prior 5-FU or gemcitabine administered concurrently with radiation as a radiosensitizer is permitted if completed >= 6 months prior to enrollment.
At least one measurable lesion per RECIST v1.1.
ECOG performance status 0 or 1; patients with ECOG PS 2 are excluded.
Absolute neutrophil count >= 1,500/uL, platelets >= 100,000/uL, hemoglobin >= 9.0 g/dL.
Serum creatinine <= 1.5x ULN or creatinine clearance >= 50 mL/min; total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN (biliary stenting/drainage permitted to achieve this).
AST/ALT <= 2.5x ULN; serum albumin >= 3.0 g/dL.
CA 19-9 must be documented at baseline for stratification; patients with Lewis antigen-negative phenotype (non-secretors) must have CA 19-9 documented as not evaluable.
Adequate biliary drainage if clinically indicated; patients with biliary obstruction must have successful drainage with total bilirubin returning to <= 1.5x ULN before first dose.

Investigator assessment that the patient's nutritional status and pain control are adequate to tolerate gemcitabine/nab-paclitaxel-based therapy; patients with uncontrolled pain requiring rapid-escalation opioids or inability to maintain adequate oral intake despite supportive measures should not be enrolled.

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Metastatic disease (Stage IV); patients with peritoneal carcinomatosis, liver metastases, lung metastases, or any distant metastatic site are excluded.
Known BRCA1/BRCA2 germline mutation eligible for PARP inhibitor maintenance; such patients should be directed to PARP-inhibitor-containing trials.
Active or history of pancreatitis within the past 12 months unrelated to the underlying malignancy.
Known peripheral neuropathy >= Grade 2.
Active or poorly controlled diabetes mellitus with HbA1c > 9.0% despite medical management; patients with well-controlled diabetes (HbA1c <= 9.0%) are eligible.
Significant cardiovascular disease within 6 months (MI, unstable angina, NYHA Class III-IV CHF, CVA).
Active venous thromboembolism requiring dose-adjustment of anticoagulation within 14 days prior to first dose; stable anticoagulation at therapeutic doses is permitted.
Major surgery within 4 weeks of first dose; surgical placement of biliary stent is not considered major surgery.

Investigator judgment that the patient has a coexisting medical condition that would substantially increase the risk of gemcitabine-related pulmonary toxicity (e.g., pre-existing interstitial lung disease, significant pulmonary fibrosis, or baseline oxygen dependence).
Concurrent enrollment in another interventional clinical trial.

trial_name: CLARITY-RCC: A Randomized, Open-Label, Phase 3 Study of TKI-9920 (a Novel Multi-Kinase Inhibitor) Plus Nivolumab Versus Sunitinib Monotherapy as First-Line Treatment in Patients with Advanced or Metastatic Renal Cell Carcinoma with Clear Cell Component

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Age >= 18 years.
Histologically confirmed renal cell carcinoma with a clear cell component (pure clear cell or mixed histology with predominant clear cell features >= 50%); non-clear cell RCC is excluded.
Advanced (not amenable to curative surgery or radiation) or metastatic disease (Stage IV).
No prior systemic therapy for advanced or metastatic RCC; prior neoadjuvant or adjuvant therapy is permitted if completed >= 6 months prior to documented recurrence/metastasis.
IMDC risk score must be documented at screening (favorable, intermediate, or poor); all risk categories are eligible but used for stratification.
At least one measurable lesion per RECIST v1.1.
Karnofsky Performance Status >= 70%.
Absolute neutrophil count >= 1,500/uL, platelets >= 100,000/uL, hemoglobin >= 9.0 g/dL.
Serum creatinine <= 1.5x ULN or creatinine clearance >= 40 mL/min; AST/ALT <= 3x ULN; total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN.
Thyroid function: TSH within normal limits or adequately controlled on thyroid replacement therapy with stable dose >= 4 weeks.
LVEF >= 50% by echocardiogram or MUGA scan within 28 days of first dose.

Blood pressure adequately controlled (systolic < 150 mmHg and diastolic < 90 mmHg) on stable antihypertensive regimen for >= 1 week prior to first dose; patients with borderline hypertension may be enrolled at investigator discretion if close monitoring is planned.
Female patients of childbearing potential must have negative pregnancy test and agree to effective contraception through 5 months after last dose of nivolumab.

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Non-clear cell RCC histology (pure papillary, chromophobe, collecting duct, medullary, or unclassified without clear cell component).
Prior TKI therapy (sunitinib, pazopanib, cabozantinib, axitinib, lenvatinib, or other VEGFR-TKI) in any setting.
Prior immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy (anti-PD-1, anti-PD-L1, or anti-CTLA-4) for RCC in any setting.
Untreated or symptomatic brain metastases; treated and stable brain metastases permitted if asymptomatic, off corticosteroids >= 2 weeks, and confirmed stable on imaging >= 4 weeks after treatment.
History of GI perforation, fistula, or intra-abdominal abscess within 6 months.

Clinically significant cardiovascular disease: MI within 6 months, unstable angina, NYHA Class III-IV heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension (systolic >= 160 mmHg or diastolic >= 100 mmHg despite optimal therapy), or arterial thromboembolic event within 6 months.
Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression within 2 years; replacement therapy permitted.
QTcF > 480 ms at screening; concomitant use of medications known to prolong QTc that cannot be discontinued or switched.
Known hepatitis B or C with active replication; HIV with detectable viral load or CD4 < 200.
Proteinuria >= 3+ on dipstick or urine protein/creatinine ratio > 1.0; 24-hour urine collection showing protein > 1 g/day.
Concurrent enrollment in another interventional trial; prior investigational agent within 28 days.

trial_name: OPAL-ADC: A Phase 2, Open-Label, Single-Arm Study Evaluating the Efficacy and Safety of Antibody-Drug Conjugate FRa-DXd (OVR-7782) in Patients with Platinum-Resistant Epithelial Ovarian, Fallopian Tube, or Primary Peritoneal Cancer Expressing Folate Receptor Alpha

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Age >= 18 years.
Histologically confirmed epithelial ovarian cancer, fallopian tube cancer, or primary peritoneal cancer of high-grade serous, endometrioid, or clear cell histology.

Platinum-resistant disease, defined as disease progression within 6 months of completing the last platinum-containing regimen; patients with primary platinum-refractory disease (progression during or within 4 weeks of completing first-line platinum therapy) are also eligible.
Folate receptor alpha (FRa) expression confirmed by IHC on archival or fresh tumor tissue: membrane staining score >= 2+ in >= 25% of tumor cells using a validated FRa assay.
Received 1-3 prior lines of systemic therapy for advanced disease (including the platinum-based regimen defining resistance); patients with > 3 prior lines are excluded.
At least one measurable lesion per RECIST v1.1 or evaluable disease per GCIG CA-125 criteria.
ECOG performance status 0 or 1.
Absolute neutrophil count >= 1,500/uL, platelets >= 100,000/uL, hemoglobin >= 9.0 g/dL.
Serum creatinine <= 1.5x ULN or eGFR >= 50 mL/min/1.73m2; AST/ALT <= 3x ULN; total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN.
LVEF >= 50% by echocardiogram within 28 days of first dose (due to potential cardiac effects of DXd payload).
Resolution of all toxicities from prior therapy to <= Grade 1 (except alopecia and Grade 2 peripheral neuropathy).
Investigator assessment that the patient can tolerate the planned treatment regimen given the cumulative toxicity burden from prior lines of therapy.

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Low-grade serous, mucinous, or carcinosarcoma histology.
Prior treatment with any antibody-drug conjugate with a topoisomerase I inhibitor payload (e.g., mirvetuximab soravtansine permitted; prior trastuzumab deruxtecan excluded).
History of interstitial lung disease (ILD) or pneumonitis that required systemic corticosteroids; drug-induced ILD/pneumonitis from prior therapy.
Active bowel obstruction or clinically significant ascites requiring paracentesis more frequently than once every 2 weeks.
Active corneal disease or history of corneal transplantation (due to FRa expression in corneal epithelium).
Known active CNS metastases; treated and stable brain metastases permitted if off corticosteroids >= 2 weeks.
Significant cardiac disease within 6 months or LVEF < 50%.
Known hypersensitivity to any component of OVR-7782 formulation.
Concurrent enrollment in another interventional trial.
Pregnant or breastfeeding; female patients must have negative pregnancy test within 7 days of first dose.

trial_name: NOVA-GLIO: A Phase 1/2, Open-Label, Dose-Escalation and Expansion Study of Novel Radiosensitizer NRS-1105 Administered Concurrently with Standard Radiation Therapy in Patients with Recurrent Glioblastoma Following Prior Temozolomide-Based Chemoradiation

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Age >= 18 years.
Histologically confirmed glioblastoma (WHO Grade 4) at initial diagnosis or at recurrence; IDH-wildtype glioblastoma is required for the Phase 2 expansion cohort.
First or second recurrence after prior standard-of-care treatment including maximal safe surgical resection (if feasible) and temozolomide-based chemoradiation (Stupp protocol or equivalent).

Recurrence confirmed by contrast-enhanced MRI demonstrating measurable disease per RANO criteria (at least one enhancing lesion with bidimensional measurement >= 10 mm x 10 mm); pseudoprogression must be excluded by advanced imaging (perfusion MRI, MR spectroscopy) or interval imaging at investigator discretion.
Karnofsky Performance Status >= 60%.
Stable or decreasing corticosteroid dose (dexamethasone <= 4 mg/day or equivalent) for >= 5 days prior to baseline MRI.
Adequate interval from prior therapies: >= 12 weeks from prior radiation, >= 4 weeks from cytotoxic chemotherapy, >= 2 weeks from targeted/biologic therapy, >= 4 weeks from bevacizumab.
Absolute neutrophil count >= 1,500/uL, platelets >= 100,000/uL, hemoglobin >= 10.0 g/dL.
Serum creatinine <= 1.5x ULN; AST/ALT <= 3x ULN; total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN.
MGMT promoter methylation status must be documented (methylated or unmethylated); used for stratification in Phase 2 expansion.

Tumor must be amenable to re-irradiation as determined by the treating radiation oncologist, with consideration of cumulative brain dose constraints and proximity to critical structures; this assessment must be documented prior to enrollment.

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: More than 2 prior recurrences or > 2 prior lines of systemic therapy for glioblastoma.
Prior re-irradiation to the brain at any time; prior stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) to the recurrent lesion is exclusionary; SRS to a separate lesion may be permitted with sponsor approval.
Diffuse leptomeningeal gliomatosis or extensive subependymal spread.
Multifocal recurrence with more than 3 separate enhancing lesions.
Planned use of bevacizumab concurrently with study treatment; prior bevacizumab is permitted if discontinued >= 4 weeks.
Known seizure disorder not adequately controlled with anti-epileptic therapy (>= 2 seizures per month despite optimized medication).
Active systemic infection requiring IV antibiotics.
Significant medical comorbidity that, in the investigator's judgment, would preclude safe participation in a combined radiosensitizer and radiation therapy protocol.
Pregnant or breastfeeding; female patients must use effective contraception during treatment and for 6 months after last dose.

trial_name: RESOLVE-HF: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase 3 Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Novel Cardiac Myosin Activator CMA-3310 in Patients with Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction (HFrEF) and Left Ventricular Ejection Fraction <= 35%

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Age >= 18 years.
Diagnosis of chronic heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF) for >= 3 months prior to screening.
LVEF <= 35% by echocardiogram performed within 30 days of randomization; if discrepant with a prior measurement, the most recent study-qualifying echo takes precedence.

NYHA functional class II or III at screening; patients with NYHA Class IV are excluded; patients transitioning between Class II and III within the screening period are eligible based on investigator assessment of predominant class.
NT-proBNP >= 400 pg/mL (or BNP >= 100 pg/mL) at screening; for patients with atrial fibrillation, NT-proBNP must be >= 900 pg/mL (or BNP >= 300 pg/mL).

On stable, optimized guideline-directed medical therapy (GDMT) for HFrEF for >= 4 weeks prior to randomization, including maximally tolerated doses of ACEi/ARB/ARNI, beta-blocker, and MRA, unless documented intolerance or contraindication; SGLT2 inhibitor use documented but not mandated.
At least one heart failure hospitalization or urgent IV diuretic visit within the past 12 months, or persistent symptoms (KCCQ-TSS < 75) despite optimized GDMT.
eGFR >= 20 mL/min/1.73m2 (CKD-EPI).
Serum potassium 3.5-5.5 mEq/L at screening.
Hemoglobin >= 9.0 g/dL.
Systolic blood pressure >= 90 mmHg at screening and randomization.
Female patients of childbearing potential must have negative pregnancy test and agree to effective contraception.

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Heart failure due to uncorrected primary valvular disease, hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, restrictive cardiomyopathy, constrictive pericarditis, active myocarditis, or congenital heart disease.
Acute decompensated heart failure requiring IV inotropes, vasopressors, or mechanical circulatory support within 2 weeks of randomization.
Cardiac resynchronization therapy (CRT) device implanted within 3 months of screening, or planned CRT implantation during the study; stable CRT for >= 3 months is permitted.
Myocardial infarction, coronary revascularization (PCI or CABG), or cardiac surgery within 3 months of screening.
Stroke or TIA within 3 months of screening.
Sustained ventricular tachycardia or ventricular fibrillation within 3 months, unless an ICD is in place and functional.
Heart rate < 50 bpm at screening (in absence of pacemaker) or symptomatic bradycardia.
Severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh C) or AST/ALT > 3x ULN.

Planned cardiac transplantation or left ventricular assist device (LVAD) implantation during the study period; patients on transplant waitlist with anticipated wait time > 12 months may be considered after sponsor discussion.
Concurrent enrollment in another interventional trial involving a heart failure therapeutic agent.

trial_name: AFFIRM-AF: A Phase 2, Randomized, Sham-Controlled Study Evaluating Catheter Ablation Plus Adjunctive Anti-Inflammatory Agent COL-4088 Versus Catheter Ablation Plus Placebo for the Treatment of Persistent Atrial Fibrillation

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Age >= 18 and <= 80 years.

Documented persistent atrial fibrillation (AF episode lasting > 7 days, or requiring cardioversion after >= 48 hours) with at least one episode documented on 12-lead ECG, Holter monitor, or implantable cardiac monitor within the past 12 months.
Symptomatic AF: EHRA symptom score >= IIa despite rate control therapy, or patient intolerant of at least one rate control agent.
Failed or intolerant of at least one Class I or Class III antiarrhythmic drug.
Scheduled for first-time catheter ablation for AF (pulmonary vein isolation); patients with prior ablation are excluded.
Left atrial diameter <= 55 mm by echocardiography (parasternal long-axis view) or left atrial volume index <= 48 mL/m2.
LVEF >= 40% by echocardiogram within 30 days of the ablation procedure.
Adequate anticoagulation for >= 3 weeks prior to ablation, or TEE demonstrating absence of left atrial appendage thrombus within 48 hours prior to procedure.
eGFR >= 30 mL/min/1.73m2.
hsCRP must be measured at baseline for stratification; elevated hsCRP does not preclude enrollment.

Investigator's assessment that the patient's atrial fibrillation pattern and substrate are amenable to catheter ablation, considering the duration of continuous AF (patients with continuous AF > 3 years require sponsor approval).

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Long-standing persistent AF (continuous AF > 12 months without any documented sinus rhythm episode).
Prior surgical or catheter-based AF ablation procedure.
Known left atrial appendage thrombus identified on pre-procedural imaging.
Severe valvular heart disease (severe mitral stenosis, mechanical prosthetic valve, or severe mitral regurgitation with planned surgical intervention).
NYHA Class III or IV heart failure or LVEF < 40%.

Active systemic inflammatory or autoimmune disease (e.g., rheumatoid arthritis, lupus, inflammatory bowel disease) requiring systemic anti-inflammatory or immunosuppressive therapy; such conditions would confound CRP-based endpoints.
Active peptic ulcer disease, history of GI perforation, or known contraindication to colchicine-class agents.
Hepatic impairment with AST/ALT > 2x ULN or total bilirubin > 1.5x ULN.
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or unwillingness to use effective contraception.

trial_name: STELLAR-PAH: A Phase 2, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study of Novel Selective Endothelin-A Receptor Antagonist ERA-7550 in Patients with Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension WHO Functional Class II-III on Background PAH Therapy

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Age 18-75 years.

Confirmed diagnosis of WHO Group 1 pulmonary arterial hypertension (idiopathic, heritable, drug/toxin-induced, or associated with connective tissue disease, congenital heart disease with simple systemic-to-pulmonary shunt, or HIV); other WHO groups are excluded.
WHO Functional Class II or III at screening.

Right heart catheterization (RHC) within 6 months of screening confirming: mean pulmonary arterial pressure (mPAP) >= 25 mmHg at rest, pulmonary artery wedge pressure (PAWP) <= 15 mmHg, and pulmonary vascular resistance (PVR) >= 3 Wood units.
6-minute walk distance (6MWD) between 150 and 500 meters at screening, with a repeat test within 15% of the first value.

On stable background PAH-specific therapy (PDE5 inhibitor and/or prostacyclin pathway agent) at unchanged doses for >= 12 weeks prior to randomization; patients on background endothelin receptor antagonist therapy are excluded.
NT-proBNP >= 150 pg/mL at screening.
AST/ALT <= 2x ULN; total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN.
Hemoglobin >= 10.0 g/dL for males, >= 9.0 g/dL for females.

Female patients of childbearing potential must have a negative pregnancy test and agree to dual contraception (hormonal + barrier) during treatment and for 3 months after last dose due to potential teratogenic effects of endothelin receptor antagonists.

Investigator assessment that the patient's functional limitation is primarily due to PAH and not significantly attributable to deconditioning, obesity (BMI > 40), musculoskeletal disease, or other comorbidities that limit exercise capacity.

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: WHO Functional Class I or Class IV at screening.

Current treatment with any endothelin receptor antagonist (bosentan, ambrisentan, macitentan) within 4 weeks of randomization; prior ERA use with documented intolerance or failure is permitted if washout of >= 4 weeks has been completed.
Pulmonary hypertension due to left heart disease (WHO Group 2), lung disease (WHO Group 3), chronic thromboembolic PH (WHO Group 4), or other mechanisms (WHO Group 5).
Clinically significant restrictive (FVC < 60% predicted) or obstructive (FEV1/FVC < 0.60) lung disease.
Systemic systolic blood pressure < 90 mmHg at screening.
Severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh B or C) or baseline AST/ALT > 3x ULN.
Hemoglobin < 9.0 g/dL or known hereditary hemoglobinopathy (e.g., sickle cell disease) that could affect 6MWD interpretation.
Hospitalization for right heart failure or clinical worsening of PAH (escalation of PAH therapy, IV prostacyclin initiation) within 3 months of screening.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding; endothelin receptor antagonists are teratogenic and strictly contraindicated in pregnancy.
Concurrent enrollment in another interventional PAH trial.

trial_name: DUAL-FORCE: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase 3 Study Evaluating the Efficacy and Safety of Novel Dual GIP/GLP-1 Receptor Agonist DGA-2280 in Patients with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus Inadequately Controlled on Metformin Monotherapy

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Age >= 18 years.
Diagnosis of type 2 diabetes mellitus for >= 6 months prior to screening.
HbA1c >= 7.0% and <= 10.5% at screening, confirmed by central laboratory.
On stable dose of metformin >= 1,000 mg/day (or maximum tolerated dose with documented inability to increase) for >= 8 weeks prior to screening.

No other glucose-lowering medications (sulfonylureas, SGLT2 inhibitors, DPP-4 inhibitors, thiazolidinediones, insulin, or any GLP-1 receptor agonist) within 8 weeks prior to screening; patients who discontinued such agents >= 8 weeks prior are eligible.
BMI >= 25 kg/m2 and <= 45 kg/m2 at screening.
Fasting C-peptide >= 1.0 ng/mL to confirm residual beta-cell function.
eGFR >= 45 mL/min/1.73m2 (CKD-EPI) at screening.
Fasting triglycerides <= 500 mg/dL at screening.
Willing and able to self-inject subcutaneous medication or have a caregiver capable of administering injections.
Stable body weight (self-reported variation < 5% within 3 months prior to screening).
Female patients of childbearing potential must have a negative pregnancy test and agree to effective contraception through 2 months after last dose.

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Type 1 diabetes mellitus, latent autoimmune diabetes in adults (LADA), or secondary diabetes (pancreatitis, pancreatectomy, Cushing's syndrome).
History of diabetic ketoacidosis within 6 months of screening.
Severe hypoglycemia (requiring third-party assistance) within 6 months of screening.
History of acute or chronic pancreatitis; lipase or amylase > 3x ULN at screening.
Personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma (MTC) or Multiple Endocrine Neoplasia syndrome type 2 (MEN 2).
History of bariatric surgery (gastric bypass, sleeve gastrectomy, adjustable gastric band) or planned bariatric procedure during the study.
Severe gastroparesis or other significant GI disorder that may affect drug absorption, in the investigator's clinical judgment.
NYHA Class III-IV heart failure or hospitalization for heart failure within 6 months.
Proliferative diabetic retinopathy or macular edema requiring acute treatment.
Concurrent enrollment in another interventional diabetes trial or use of investigational glucose-lowering agent within 3 months.

trial_name: FIBROSOLVE: A Phase 2, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study of Antifibrotic Agent AFB-6640 in Patients with Biopsy-Confirmed Non-Alcoholic Steatohepatitis (NASH) and Liver Fibrosis Stage F2-F3

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Age 18-75 years.

Liver biopsy within 6 months of screening (or during screening) demonstrating NASH with NAS (NAFLD Activity Score) >= 4 with at least 1 point each for steatosis, lobular inflammation, and hepatocyte ballooning.
Fibrosis stage F2 or F3 on liver biopsy per NASH CRN scoring system; patients with F0, F1, or F4 (cirrhosis) are excluded.
BMI >= 25 kg/m2 (or >= 23 kg/m2 for Asian patients).
ALT >= 1.5x ULN at screening to ensure active disease.
If diabetic, HbA1c <= 9.5% on stable glucose-lowering therapy (unchanged for >= 3 months); non-diabetic patients eligible without HbA1c threshold.
Alcohol consumption must be < 21 standard drinks/week for men and < 14 drinks/week for women for at least 2 years prior to screening, confirmed by patient history and AUDIT-C score.
Platelet count >= 100,000/uL; INR <= 1.3.
eGFR >= 45 mL/min/1.73m2.
Stable use of vitamin E, pioglitazone, or obeticholic acid is permitted if dose unchanged for >= 6 months; initiation of these agents during the study is prohibited.
Biopsy adequacy is subject to central pathology review; biopsies with < 15 mm total length or < 6 portal tracts may be deemed inadequate and require repeat biopsy at the investigator's discretion.

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Cirrhosis (fibrosis stage F4 on biopsy), clinical evidence of cirrhosis (ascites, variceal bleeding, hepatic encephalopathy), or FibroScan liver stiffness >= 14.0 kPa.

Other chronic liver disease: autoimmune hepatitis, primary biliary cholangitis, primary sclerosing cholangitis, Wilson disease, hemochromatosis (unless treated), alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency with liver involvement.
Active HBV (HBsAg positive) or HCV (detectable HCV RNA) infection; prior HCV with documented SVR is permitted.

Significant alcohol use (>= 21 drinks/week for men, >= 14 drinks/week for women) at any point within the past 2 years; prior history of alcohol use disorder is exclusionary unless documented sustained sobriety >= 3 years.

Use of medications known to cause hepatic steatosis (amiodarone, systemic corticosteroids > 7 days, methotrexate, tamoxifen, valproic acid) within 3 months of screening unless on stable dose and clinically necessary.
History of liver transplantation.
Total bilirubin > 2x ULN (unless documented Gilbert syndrome).
Known hepatocellular carcinoma or other active malignancy.
Bariatric surgery within 5 years of screening or planned bariatric surgery during the study; weight loss > 10% body weight within 6 months of screening.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding; effective contraception required during treatment and for 3 months after last dose.

trial_name: TRANSFORM-MET: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase 3 Study of Novel Central Appetite Regulator APR-8120 in Patients with Obesity and Metabolic Syndrome

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Age >= 18 years.
BMI >= 30 kg/m2, or BMI >= 27 kg/m2 with at least one weight-related comorbidity (hypertension, dyslipidemia, type 2 diabetes, obstructive sleep apnea).

Metabolic syndrome defined by presence of at least 3 of 5 criteria: waist circumference >= 102 cm (men) or >= 88 cm (women), fasting triglycerides >= 150 mg/dL or on triglyceride-lowering therapy, HDL-C < 40 mg/dL (men) or < 50 mg/dL (women) or on HDL-raising therapy, blood pressure >= 130/85 mmHg or on antihypertensive therapy, fasting glucose >= 100 mg/dL or on glucose-lowering therapy.
Stable body weight (self-reported variation < 5 kg) within 3 months prior to screening.
If on antihypertensive, lipid-lowering, or glucose-lowering medications, doses must be stable for >= 3 months prior to screening.
eGFR >= 30 mL/min/1.73m2.
AST/ALT <= 3x ULN; total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN.
Fasting triglycerides <= 500 mg/dL.
HbA1c <= 10.0% if diabetic; if on insulin, total daily dose must be stable (variation < 20%) for >= 8 weeks.
Willing to participate in standardized lifestyle counseling (diet + exercise) provided to all study participants.
Investigator assessment that the patient's obesity is not primarily attributable to a treatable endocrine disorder (untreated hypothyroidism, Cushing's syndrome) or known monogenic obesity syndrome.
Female patients of childbearing potential must have negative pregnancy test and agree to effective contraception during treatment and for 2 months after last dose.

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: History of bariatric surgery (gastric bypass, sleeve gastrectomy, adjustable band, biliopancreatic diversion) or planned bariatric procedure during the study.

Current or recent (within 3 months) use of GLP-1 receptor agonists (semaglutide, liraglutide, tirzepatide, dulaglutide), anti-obesity medications (phentermine, orlistat, naltrexone/bupropion), or other investigational weight-loss agents.
Uncontrolled type 2 diabetes with HbA1c > 10.0% or requiring intensification of glucose-lowering therapy at screening.
Type 1 diabetes mellitus.
History of major depressive disorder with suicidal ideation within the past 2 years, or any lifetime history of suicide attempt; PHQ-9 score >= 15 at screening.
Active bulimia nervosa, binge-eating disorder requiring pharmacotherapy, or other significant eating disorder as determined by the investigator.
NYHA Class III-IV heart failure; MI, stroke, or TIA within 6 months.
Personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN 2 (precautionary exclusion for agents with incretin-like activity).
History of pancreatitis within 12 months or lipase > 3x ULN at screening.
Concurrent enrollment in another interventional weight-management or metabolic syndrome trial.

trial_name: JOINT-RELIEF: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase 3 Study of Novel Selective JAK1 Inhibitor JKI-5500 in Patients with Moderate-to-Severe Active Rheumatoid Arthritis Who Have Had an Inadequate Response to Methotrexate

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Age >= 18 years.
Diagnosis of rheumatoid arthritis (RA) per 2010 ACR/EULAR classification criteria for at least 6 months prior to screening.
Active disease at screening defined by >= 6 tender joints (68-joint count), >= 6 swollen joints (66-joint count), and hsCRP >= 5 mg/L or ESR >= 28 mm/hr.
DAS28-CRP >= 3.2 at screening (moderate or high disease activity).

Inadequate response to methotrexate (MTX): on stable dose of MTX 15-25 mg/week (or >= 10 mg/week with documented intolerance to higher doses) for >= 12 weeks, with stable dose for >= 4 weeks prior to baseline.
Positive for rheumatoid factor (RF) and/or anti-citrullinated protein antibody (anti-CCP) at screening, or documented erosive disease on hand/wrist/foot radiographs.
Hemoglobin >= 9.0 g/dL, ANC >= 1,000/uL, platelets >= 100,000/uL, lymphocyte count >= 750/uL.
AST/ALT <= 1.5x ULN; eGFR >= 40 mL/min/1.73m2.
Lipid panel (total cholesterol, LDL, HDL, triglycerides) must be documented at baseline for cardiovascular risk stratification.
Female patients of childbearing potential must have negative pregnancy test and agree to effective contraception during treatment and for 4 weeks after last dose.
Investigator's assessment that the patient's functional limitations are primarily attributable to RA disease activity rather than irreversible joint damage.

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Prior use of any JAK inhibitor (tofacitinib, baricitinib, upadacitinib, filgotinib, or investigational JAK inhibitor).

Use of biologic DMARDs (TNF inhibitors, IL-6 inhibitors, abatacept, rituximab, or other biologic) within the following washout periods: 4 weeks for etanercept, 8 weeks for infliximab/adalimumab/certolizumab/golimumab/tocilizumab/sarilumab/abatacept, 6 months for rituximab (with confirmed B-cell reconstitution).

Use of conventional synthetic DMARDs other than methotrexate (leflunomide, sulfasalazine, hydroxychloroquine) unless on stable dose >= 12 weeks and continued through the study; leflunomide requires washout or cholestyramine elimination if discontinued.
Prednisone > 10 mg/day (or equivalent corticosteroid) or unstable corticosteroid dose within 4 weeks of baseline.

History of or current venous thromboembolism (DVT, PE) or arterial thrombotic event (MI, stroke) within 12 months; age >= 65 years with at least one additional cardiovascular risk factor (current smoking, hypertension, diabetes) requires sponsor discussion given JAK inhibitor class safety signals.
Known current malignancy or history of malignancy within 5 years, except adequately treated non-melanoma skin cancer or cervical CIS.

Active or latent tuberculosis (positive QuantiFERON-TB Gold or tuberculin skin test >= 5 mm induration) unless the patient has completed >= 1 month of an accepted TB prophylaxis regimen prior to first dose and agrees to complete the full course.
Active herpes zoster or history of disseminated herpes zoster; patients must have completed herpes zoster vaccination (recombinant) >= 4 weeks prior to first dose if not previously vaccinated.
Known HIV, active HBV (HBsAg positive), or active HCV (detectable RNA) infection; prior HCV with SVR is permitted.
Lymphocyte count < 750/uL at screening.
Concurrent enrollment in another interventional RA trial.

trial_name: LUSTRE-SLE: A Phase 2, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study of B-Cell Targeted Biologic BCD-4470 in Patients with Active Systemic Lupus Erythematosus Despite Standard-of-Care Therapy

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Age >= 18 years.
Diagnosis of systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) fulfilling the 2019 EULAR/ACR classification criteria, diagnosed >= 6 months prior to screening.
Active disease at screening: SLEDAI-2K score >= 6, with at least 4 points from clinical criteria (excluding serological criteria alone).
Positive anti-dsDNA antibody (>= 30 IU/mL by Farr assay or equivalent) or low complement (C3 < LLN and/or C4 < LLN) at screening.

On stable standard-of-care therapy for >= 30 days prior to baseline, which must include at least one of: antimalarial (hydroxychloroquine), immunosuppressive (azathioprine, mycophenolate, methotrexate), or oral corticosteroid (prednisone <= 20 mg/day or equivalent).
If on corticosteroids, prednisone dose must be <= 20 mg/day and stable for >= 2 weeks prior to baseline.
ANC >= 1,000/uL (or >= 500/uL if attributable to SLE per investigator judgment), platelets >= 50,000/uL, hemoglobin >= 8.0 g/dL.
eGFR >= 30 mL/min/1.73m2; urine protein/creatinine ratio <= 2.0 mg/mg (patients with active lupus nephritis Class V with higher proteinuria may be permitted after sponsor discussion).
Female patients of childbearing potential must have negative pregnancy test and agree to effective contraception during treatment and for 6 months after last dose.
Willingness to maintain current SLE standard-of-care medications at stable doses through Week 24 (unless dose reduction is required for safety).

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Active severe lupus nephritis (Class III or IV with recent flare within 3 months) requiring induction immunosuppressive therapy; stable Class V membranous nephritis on maintenance therapy may be permitted.
Active neuropsychiatric lupus (seizures, psychosis, transverse myelitis, cerebral vasculitis) within 12 months.
Prior treatment with B-cell depleting therapy (rituximab, obinutuzumab, ocrelizumab) within 12 months, or evidence of persistent B-cell depletion (CD19 count < LLN) regardless of timing.
Prior treatment with belimumab within 3 months or anifrolumab within 3 months.
Cyclophosphamide use within 6 months of baseline.
Active or latent tuberculosis; active serious infection requiring hospitalization or IV antimicrobials within 4 weeks of baseline.
Known HIV, active HBV, or active HCV infection.
History of anaphylaxis to any biologic therapy.
Known IgA deficiency (serum IgA < 10 mg/dL), which increases the risk of severe infections with B-cell targeted therapy.
Concurrent enrollment in another interventional SLE trial or receipt of investigational agent within 28 days or 5 half-lives.

trial_name: HORIZON-CD: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase 3 Induction and Maintenance Study of Anti-IL-23p19 Monoclonal Antibody ILB-2290 in Patients with Moderately to Severely Active Crohn's Disease

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Age >= 18 years.
Diagnosis of Crohn's disease (CD) for >= 3 months prior to screening, confirmed by clinical, endoscopic, radiographic, and/or histologic criteria.
Moderately to severely active CD at screening defined by CDAI score 220-450.

Endoscopic evidence of active disease: SES-CD (Simple Endoscopic Score for Crohn's Disease) >= 6 (or >= 4 for patients with isolated ileal disease) on centrally-read ileocolonoscopy performed within 8 weeks of baseline.

Inadequate response, loss of response, or intolerance to at least one of the following: conventional therapy (corticosteroids, immunomodulators such as azathioprine, 6-mercaptopurine, or methotrexate) or biologic therapy (anti-TNF agents, vedolizumab, ustekinumab).
If on oral corticosteroids, dose must be <= 20 mg/day prednisone equivalent and stable for >= 2 weeks prior to baseline; budesonide <= 9 mg/day permitted.
If on immunomodulators (azathioprine, 6-MP, methotrexate), dose must be stable for >= 8 weeks prior to baseline.
Fecal calprotectin >= 250 ug/g or CRP >= 5 mg/L at screening as objective evidence of active inflammation.
Hemoglobin >= 8.5 g/dL, ANC >= 1,500/uL, platelets >= 100,000/uL.
AST/ALT <= 2x ULN; serum creatinine <= 1.5x ULN.
Female patients of childbearing potential must have negative pregnancy test and agree to effective contraception during treatment and for 15 weeks after last dose.
Investigator assessment that the patient's symptoms are attributable to active CD inflammation and not primarily due to stricture-related obstruction, short bowel syndrome, or bile salt diarrhea.

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Diagnosis of ulcerative colitis or indeterminate colitis.
Known symptomatic bowel stricture requiring imminent surgical intervention or endoscopic dilation.
Short bowel syndrome (history of extensive intestinal resection with < 200 cm of remaining small bowel).
Active perianal or intra-abdominal abscess; perianal fistulae without abscess are permitted.
Current ileostomy or colostomy.
Prior treatment with any anti-IL-23p19 antibody (risankizumab, guselkumab, tildrakizumab, mirikizumab); prior ustekinumab (anti-IL-12/23p40) is permitted with appropriate washout (>= 8 weeks).

Anti-TNF therapy (infliximab, adalimumab, certolizumab) or vedolizumab within 8 weeks of baseline; for anti-TNF agents with detectable drug levels, washout is extended until drug level is below the assay limit of quantification.
Active or latent tuberculosis (positive QuantiFERON-TB Gold) unless prophylaxis initiated >= 3 weeks prior to first dose with plan to complete full course.
Stool positive for Clostridioides difficile toxin at screening.
Known active hepatitis B or C, or HIV infection with CD4 < 200.
Concurrent enrollment in another interventional IBD trial.

trial_name: BREATHE-CLEAR: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase 3 Study of Anti-TSLP Monoclonal Antibody TSL-4480 as Add-On Therapy in Patients with Severe Uncontrolled Eosinophilic Asthma Despite Medium-to-High Dose Inhaled Corticosteroids Plus a Second Controller

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Age >= 12 years (adolescents 12-17 with body weight >= 40 kg are eligible).
Physician-diagnosed asthma for >= 12 months prior to screening per GINA guidelines.

Severe uncontrolled asthma defined by: receiving medium-to-high dose ICS (>= 500 mcg fluticasone propionate equivalent/day) plus at least one additional controller (LABA, LTRA, LAMA, or theophylline) at stable doses for >= 3 months prior to screening.
Blood eosinophil count >= 300 cells/uL at screening or >= 150 cells/uL with FeNO >= 25 ppb.
Pre-bronchodilator FEV1 40-85% predicted at screening.

Documented reversibility (FEV1 increase >= 12% and >= 200 mL after 400 mcg salbutamol) at screening or within the past 12 months, or positive methacholine challenge (PC20 <= 16 mg/mL) within 12 months.
>= 2 asthma exacerbations requiring systemic corticosteroids (oral/injectable, >= 3 days) or >= 1 hospitalization/ED visit for asthma within the past 12 months, despite controller therapy.
ACQ-6 (Asthma Control Questionnaire) score >= 1.5 at screening.
Demonstrated adherence to controller medications (>= 80% as assessed by device counter review or pharmacy refill records) during the run-in period.
Non-smoker or ex-smoker with smoking history < 10 pack-years and no smoking within the past 12 months.
Female patients of childbearing potential must have negative pregnancy test and agree to effective contraception during treatment and for 120 days after last dose.

Investigator assessment that the patient's uncontrolled symptoms are not primarily attributable to poor inhaler technique, non-adherence, or untreated comorbidities (allergic rhinitis, GERD, vocal cord dysfunction).

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Current use of biologic therapy for asthma (omalizumab, mepolizumab, reslizumab, benralizumab, dupilumab, tezepelumab) or use within the following washout periods: 4 months for omalizumab, 5 months for mepolizumab/reslizumab, 4 months for benralizumab, 2 months for dupilumab.
Maintenance oral corticosteroids (daily use for > 14 consecutive days within 4 weeks of screening); burst courses for exacerbations are permitted if completed >= 2 weeks before baseline.
Current smoker or smoking history >= 10 pack-years.

Diagnosis of COPD, bronchiectasis, allergic bronchopulmonary aspergillosis (ABPA), or other chronic respiratory disease other than asthma that, in the investigator's judgment, is the primary cause of symptoms.
Respiratory tract infection (upper or lower) requiring antibiotics or antivirals within 2 weeks of baseline.
Known parasitic (helminth) infection within 6 months of screening or ongoing risk requiring prophylaxis.
Known immunodeficiency or immunosuppressive therapy other than stable low-dose oral corticosteroids and inhaled corticosteroids.
Known hypersensitivity to any component of the TSL-4480 formulation.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Concurrent enrollment in another interventional asthma trial.

trial_name: FIBREHOLD: A Phase 2, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study Evaluating Novel Antifibrotic Agent NFA-3320 in Patients with Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis on Stable Background Antifibrotic Therapy

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Age >= 40 years.

Diagnosis of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) per 2022 ATS/ERS/JRS/ALAT guidelines, confirmed by high-resolution CT pattern of usual interstitial pneumonia (UIP) or probable UIP, with or without surgical lung biopsy confirmation.

HRCT pattern reviewed and confirmed by a central radiology committee; cases with probable UIP on CT that have not undergone surgical biopsy require multidisciplinary discussion (MDD) confirmation documented in source records.
FVC >= 45% predicted at screening.
DLCO (corrected for hemoglobin) >= 25% predicted at screening.

On stable dose of approved background antifibrotic therapy (pirfenidone or nintedanib) for >= 8 weeks prior to screening; dose reductions for tolerability are acceptable if the dose has been stable for >= 4 weeks prior to baseline.
FVC decline of >= 5% relative (or >= 100 mL absolute) within the past 12-24 months despite background antifibrotic therapy, documented by serial pulmonary function tests.
Able to perform reliable, reproducible spirometry per ATS/ERS standards.
6-minute walk distance >= 150 meters at screening with SpO2 nadir >= 80% on room air or supplemental oxygen.
AST/ALT <= 3x ULN; total bilirubin <= 1.5x ULN; eGFR >= 30 mL/min/1.73m2.
Female patients of childbearing potential must have negative pregnancy test and agree to effective contraception.

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Known cause of pulmonary fibrosis other than IPF (e.g., connective tissue disease-associated ILD, hypersensitivity pneumonitis, drug-induced ILD, asbestosis, sarcoidosis).
Obstructive lung disease: FEV1/FVC ratio < 0.70 at screening (post-bronchodilator).

Current treatment with systemic corticosteroids > 10 mg/day prednisone equivalent or systemic immunosuppressives (azathioprine, mycophenolate, cyclophosphamide) for IPF; these agents should have been discontinued >= 4 weeks prior to screening.
Acute exacerbation of IPF within 3 months of screening.
Listed for lung transplantation or expected to receive lung transplant within 12 months.
Significant emphysema (> 15% of lung fields on HRCT) that, in the investigator's judgment, is the predominant contributor to the patient's physiologic impairment.
Active respiratory infection within 4 weeks of screening.
Severe pulmonary hypertension (estimated RVSP > 50 mmHg by echocardiogram or mean PAP > 35 mmHg by RHC).
Concurrent enrollment in another interventional IPF trial.

trial_name: TRILOGY-COPD: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Parallel-Group Phase 3 Study Comparing Triple Fixed-Dose Combination Inhaler (ICS/LABA/LAMA) Plus Adjunctive Anti-IL-5 Biologic MEP-2260 Versus Triple Inhaler Plus Placebo in Patients with Severe COPD and Frequent Eosinophilic Exacerbations

inclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Age >= 40 years.
COPD diagnosis per GOLD criteria with post-bronchodilator FEV1/FVC < 0.70 and FEV1 < 50% predicted at screening.
GOLD group E (>= 2 moderate exacerbations or >= 1 exacerbation requiring hospitalization within the past 12 months) despite triple inhaler therapy (ICS/LABA/LAMA).
Blood eosinophil count >= 300 cells/uL at screening (or >= 150 cells/uL with documented eosinophilia >= 300 cells/uL on at least one measurement within the past 12 months).
On stable triple inhaler therapy (ICS at medium-to-high dose + LABA + LAMA) for >= 3 months prior to screening.
CAT (COPD Assessment Test) score >= 10 at screening.
Smoking history >= 10 pack-years (current or former smoker).
Chest imaging (CT or X-ray) within the past 12 months with no findings suggestive of alternative diagnoses (lung malignancy, active TB, significant bronchiectasis).
Able to perform acceptable and reproducible spirometry per ATS/ERS standards.
Hemoglobin >= 9.0 g/dL; AST/ALT <= 2x ULN; eGFR >= 30 mL/min/1.73m2.
Female patients of childbearing potential must have negative pregnancy test and agree to effective contraception.

Investigator assessment that the patient's exacerbation pattern has an eosinophilic phenotype and is not primarily driven by chronic bacterial colonization, bronchiectasis, or poor medication adherence.

exclusion_criteria.criterion_text: Diagnosis of asthma or asthma-COPD overlap (ACO) as the primary respiratory condition; patients with a remote childhood history of asthma that is not currently active may be considered after sponsor discussion.
Current use of biologic therapy for any respiratory indication (mepolizumab, benralizumab, dupilumab, tezepelumab) or use within 5 half-lives of the respective agent.
COPD exacerbation or respiratory infection requiring antibiotics or systemic corticosteroids within 4 weeks of baseline; such events require a 4-week stable period before rescreening.
Maintenance oral corticosteroids (> 5 mg/day prednisone equivalent continuously for > 14 days within 4 weeks of screening).
Alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency as the primary cause of COPD.
Clinically significant bronchiectasis (involvement of >= 3 lobes on CT) as the predominant contributor to exacerbations.
Active pulmonary tuberculosis or non-tuberculous mycobacterial infection.
Known parasitic (helminth) infection within 6 months; anti-IL-5 therapy may increase susceptibility to parasitic infections.
Long-term oxygen therapy for > 15 hours/day (supplemental oxygen for exercise or nocturnal use is permitted).
Lung volume reduction surgery or lung transplant history; patients listed for transplantation are excluded.
Concurrent enrollment in another interventional COPD trial.
